title: Laptop Pro X1 - Won't Turn On / No Power
content:
## Laptop Pro X1 - Won't Turn On

**Step 1: Check Power Supply**
- Ensure the 140W USB-C charger is firmly connected to both the laptop and power outlet.
- Try a different power outlet.
- Check if the charging LED (near the USB-C port) lights up when plugged in.

**Step 2: Perform a Hard Reset**
- Disconnect the charger and all peripherals.
- Press and hold the power button for 15 seconds.
- Reconnect the charger and try powering on.

**Step 3: Check Display**
- Connect an external monitor via HDMI or Thunderbolt to rule out a display issue.
- If the external monitor works, the internal display may need service.

**Step 4: Battery Reset**
- If the laptop has been fully drained, plug in the charger and wait 15 minutes before attempting to power on.

**If none of the above works:** The device may have a hardware issue. Please contact support for a warranty claim or repair. Serial number will be needed.

title: Wireless Earbuds Z5 - Bluetooth Connection Issues
content:
## Wireless Earbuds Z5 - Bluetooth Connection Issues

**Step 1: Reset Bluetooth Pairing**
- Place both earbuds in the charging case.
- Press and hold the button on the case for 10 seconds until the LED flashes red then white.
- The earbuds are now in pairing mode. Remove them and pair again from your device's Bluetooth settings.

**Step 2: Clear Device Pairing List**
- In the TechNova Audio app, go to Settings > Bluetooth > Clear Pairing List.
- This removes all previously connected devices and allows fresh pairing.

**Step 3: Check Bluetooth Interference**
- Move away from Wi-Fi routers, microwaves, or other Bluetooth devices.
- Ensure your phone/device Bluetooth is on and discoverable.

**Step 4: Update Firmware**
- Open the TechNova Audio app and check for firmware updates.
- Outdated firmware can cause connection stability issues.

**Step 5: Check Multipoint**
- If connected to 2 devices simultaneously, audio may route to the wrong device. Disconnect one device to test.

**If issues persist:** Try the earbuds with a different device. If the problem continues, contact support.

title: Wireless Earbuds Z5 - Not Charging
content:
## Wireless Earbuds Z5 - Not Charging

**Step 1: Clean Charging Contacts**
- Remove the earbuds and clean the gold charging pins inside the case with a dry cotton swab.
- Also clean the contacts on the earbuds themselves.

**Step 2: Check the Charging Case**
- Connect the case to a USB-C charger. The case LED should pulse while charging.
- If no LED, try a different cable and power adapter.

**Step 3: Reset the Earbuds**
- Place earbuds in case, press and hold the case button for 15 seconds.
- The LEDs will flash rapidly, then turn off. This performs a full reset.

**Step 4: Check Earbud Placement**
- Ensure earbuds are seated properly in the case (you should feel a magnetic click).
- The LED should briefly flash when each earbud is placed correctly.

**Red Flashing LED:** Indicates a hardware fault with the charging circuit. This is covered under warranty.

title: Smart Watch S3 - Not Charging
content:
## Smart Watch S3 - Not Charging

**Step 1: Check Charging Cradle**
- Ensure the magnetic charging cradle is properly aligned with the back of the watch.
- The watch should snap into place magnetically.

**Step 2: Clean Contacts**
- Clean the charging contacts on the back of the watch with a dry, lint-free cloth.
- Remove any sweat, dirt, or residue.

**Step 3: Try Different Power Source**
- Use a different USB-A port or power adapter (5V/1A minimum).
- Avoid high-power fast chargers that exceed 5V/2A.

**Step 4: Restart the Watch**
- Press and hold the side button for 10 seconds until you see the TechNova logo.

**Step 5: Check for Overheating**
- If the watch is hot, let it cool down before charging. Charging is disabled above 45C for safety.

**If none of the above works:** The charging coil may be defective. Contact support for warranty service.

title: 4K Monitor U27 - No Signal / Black Screen
content:
## 4K Monitor U27 - No Signal

**Step 1: Check Cable Connection**
- Ensure the video cable (DisplayPort, HDMI, or USB-C) is firmly connected at both ends.
- Try a different cable if available.

**Step 2: Select Correct Input**
- Press the joystick button on the back of the monitor to open the OSD menu.
- Navigate to Input Source and select the correct input (DP, HDMI1, HDMI2, or USB-C).

**Step 3: Check Cable Type for 4K 144Hz**
- 4K at 144Hz requires DisplayPort 1.4 cable or HDMI 2.1 cable.
- Older cables may not support the full resolution/refresh rate.
- USB-C requires the cable to support DisplayPort Alt Mode.

**Step 4: Update Graphics Drivers**
- Ensure your GPU drivers are up to date (NVIDIA/AMD/Intel).

**Step 5: Test with Another Device**
- Connect a different computer or laptop to rule out a source issue.

**Step 6: Factory Reset**
- In the OSD menu, go to Settings > Reset All to restore default settings.

**Monitor power LED is off:** Check the power cable and press the power button on the bottom of the monitor.

title: Mechanical Keyboard K7 - Wireless Not Connecting
content:
## Mechanical Keyboard K7 - Wireless Not Connecting

**For Bluetooth:**
1. Press Fn + 1/2/3 to select a Bluetooth channel.
2. Press and hold Fn + 1/2/3 for 3 seconds until the corresponding LED blinks rapidly.
3. The keyboard is now in pairing mode. Find "TechNova K7" in your device's Bluetooth settings.

**For 2.4GHz:**
1. Plug the 2.4GHz USB dongle into your computer.
2. Press Fn + 4 to switch to 2.4GHz mode.
3. The connection should be automatic. If not, press and hold Fn + 4 for 3 seconds to re-pair.

**General Troubleshooting:**
- Ensure the keyboard is charged (check by pressing Fn + Space, LED bar shows battery level).
- Toggle the power switch on the back of the keyboard OFF then ON.
- Keep the keyboard within 10m of the receiver (Bluetooth) or 1m for initial 2.4GHz pairing.
- Remove other wireless devices that may cause interference.

**Factory Reset:**
- Press and hold Fn + Esc for 5 seconds. All LEDs will flash. This clears all Bluetooth pairings and restores default key mappings.

title: Portable Speaker M8 - No Sound
content:
## Portable Speaker M8 - No Sound

**Step 1: Check Volume**
- Press the volume up button on the speaker. A tone plays at each volume increment.
- Also check the volume on your connected device.

**Step 2: Verify Bluetooth Connection**
- Check your phone/device shows "TechNova M8" as connected.
- Try playing audio from a different app.

**Step 3: Re-pair the Speaker**
- Press and hold the Bluetooth button for 5 seconds until you hear "pairing mode."
- Forget "TechNova M8" from your device's Bluetooth list and pair again.

**Step 4: Check for Speaker Blockage**
- Ensure the speaker grille is not blocked by dirt or debris.
- If the speaker was submerged in water, shake gently to remove water from the grille and let it dry.

**Step 5: Reset the Speaker**
- Press and hold the Power + Volume Down buttons simultaneously for 10 seconds.

**Distorted Sound:** This usually occurs at very high volume. Lower the volume slightly. If distortion persists at all volumes, contact support.
